contentBlocks.original: अस्य श्रीसिद्धलक्ष्मीस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य हिरण्यगर्भ ऋषिः अनुष्टुप् छन्दः, श्रीमहाकालीमहालक्ष्मीमहासरस्वत्यो देवताः श्रीं बीजं ह्रीं शक्तिः क्लीं कीलकं मम सर्वक्लेशपीडापरिहारार्थं सर्वदुःखदारिद्र्यनाशनार्थं सर्वकार्यसिद्ध्यर्थं श्रीसिद्धिलक्ष्मीस्तोत्र पाठे विनियोगः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: asya śrīsiddhalakṣmīstōtramantrasya hiraṇyagarbha ṛṣiḥ anuṣṭup Chandaḥ, śrīmahākāḻīmahālakṣmīmahāsarasvatyō dēvatāḥ śrīṃ bījaṃ hrīṃ śaktiḥ klīṃ kīlakaṃ mama sarvaklēśapīḍāparihārārthaṃ sarvaduḥkhadāridryanāśanārthaṃ sarvakāryasiddhyarthaṃ śrīsiddhilakṣmīstōtra pāṭhē viniyōgaḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Viniyoga of the Śrī Siddha Lakṣmī Stotram: seer — Hiraṇyagarbha; meter — Anuṣṭup; deities — Śrī Mahākālī, Mahālakṣmī, Mahāsarasvatī; bīja — Śrīṃ; śakti — Hrīṃ; kīlaka — Klīṃ. Application: for removal of all afflictions and suffering, destruction of all poverty and sorrow, and accomplishment of all aims — the recitation of this Śrī Siddhalakṣmī Stotram.
contentBlocks.meanings: asya — of this; śrī-siddha-lakṣmī-stotra-mantrasya — of the Śrī Siddhalakṣmī Stotram-mantra; hiraṇyagarbha ṛṣiḥ — Hiraṇyagarbha (Brahmā) is the seer; anuṣṭup chandaḥ — Anuṣṭup is the meter; śrī-mahākālī-mahālakṣmī-mahāsarasvatyaḥ devatāḥ — the three Mahādevatās are the deities; śrīṃ bījaṃ — Śrīṃ is the seed-syllable; hrīṃ śaktiḥ — Hrīṃ is the power; klīṃ kīlakaṃ — Klīṃ is the linchpin; sarva-kleśa-pīḍā-parihārārthaṃ — for removal of all afflictions and torments; sarva-duḥkha-dāridrya-nāśanārthaṃ — for destruction of all sorrow and poverty; sarva-kārya-siddhy-arthaṃ — for accomplishment of all purposes; viniyogaḥ — application.

contentBlocks.original:
ऋष्यादिन्यासः
ॐ हिरण्यगर्भ ऋषये नमः शिरसि ।
अनुष्टुप्छन्दसे नमो मुखे ।
श्रीमहाकालीमहालक्ष्मीमहासरस्वतीदेवताभ्यो नमो हृदिः ।
श्रीं बीजाय नमो गुह्ये ।
ह्रीं शक्तये नमः पादयोः ।
क्लीं कीलकाय नमो नाभौ ।
विनियोगाय नमः सर्वाङ्गेषु ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ṛṣyādinyāsaḥ
ōṃ hiraṇyagarbha ṛṣayē namaḥ śirasi ।
anuṣṭupChandasē namō mukhē ।
śrīmahākāḻīmahālakṣmīmahāsarasvatīdēvatābhyō namō hṛdiḥ ।
śrīṃ bījāya namō guhyē ।
hrīṃ śaktayē namaḥ pādayōḥ ।
klīṃ kīlakāya namō nābhau ।
viniyōgāya namaḥ sarvāṅgēṣu ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ṛṣyādi Nyāsa (assignment of the seer and other elements): Oṃ — salutation to the seer Hiraṇyagarbha on the crown of the head. Salutation to the Anuṣṭup meter at the mouth. Salutation to the three deities Mahākālī-Mahālakṣmī-Mahāsarasvatī at the heart. Salutation to the bīja Śrīṃ at the hidden center. Salutation to the śakti Hrīṃ at the feet. Salutation to the kīlaka Klīṃ at the navel. Salutation to the application (viniyoga) throughout all limbs.
contentBlocks.meanings: ṛṣyādi-nyāsaḥ — ritual placement of the seer and other elements; ṛṣaye namaḥ śirasi — salutation to the seer on the crown; chandasē namaḥ mukhē — salutation to the meter at the mouth; dēvatābhyaḥ namaḥ hṛdi — salutation to the deities at the heart; bījāya namaḥ guhyē — salutation to the bīja at the secret center; śaktayē namaḥ pādayōḥ — salutation to the śakti at both feet; kīlakāya namaḥ nābhau — salutation to the kīlaka at the navel; viniyōgāya namaḥ sarvāṅgēṣu — salutation to the application throughout all limbs.

contentBlocks.original:
करन्यासः
ॐ श्रीं सिद्धलक्ष्म्यै अङ्गुष्ठाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ ह्रीं विष्णुतेजसे तर्जनीभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ क्लीं अमृतानन्दायै मध्यमाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ श्रीं दैत्यमालिन्यै अनामिकाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ ह्रीं तेजः प्रकाशिन्यै कनिष्ठिकाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ क्लीं ब्राह्म्यै वैष्णव्यै रुद्राण्यै करतल करपृष्ठाभ्यां नमः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karanyāsaḥ
ōṃ śrīṃ siddhalakṣmyai aṅguṣṭhābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ hrīṃ viṣṇutējasē tarjanībhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ klīṃ amṛtānandāyai madhyamābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ śrīṃ daityamālinyai anāmikābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ hrīṃ tējaḥ prakāśinyai kaniṣṭhikābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ klīṃ brāhmyai vaiṣṇavyai rudrāṇyai karatala karapṛṣṭhābhyāṃ namaḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kara Nyāsa (placement on the hands): Oṃ Śrīṃ — salutation to Siddhalakṣmī — on the two thumbs. Oṃ Hrīṃ — salutation to Viṣṇutējas — on the two forefingers. Oṃ Klīṃ — salutation to Amṛtānandā — on the two middle fingers. Oṃ Śrīṃ — salutation to Daityamālinī — on the two ring-fingers. Oṃ Hrīṃ — salutation to Tējaḥprakāśinī — on the two little fingers. Oṃ Klīṃ — salutation to Brāhmī, Vaiṣṇavī, Rudrāṇī — on the palms and backs of the hands.
contentBlocks.meanings: kara-nyāsaḥ — placement on the hands; aṅguṣṭhābhyāṃ namaḥ — salutation with/on the two thumbs; tarjanībhyāṃ namaḥ — on the two forefingers; madhyamābhyāṃ namaḥ — on the two middle fingers; anāmikābhyāṃ namaḥ — on the two ring-fingers; kaniṣṭhikābhyāṃ namaḥ — on the two little fingers; karatala-karapṛṣṭhābhyāṃ namaḥ — on the palms and backs of the hands; viṣṇutējasē — to Viṣṇu's radiance; daityamālinyai — to the garlander of demons; tējaḥprakāśinyai — to the illuminatrix of radiance.

contentBlocks.original:
अङ्गन्यासः
ॐ श्रीं सिद्धलक्ष्म्यै हृदयाय नमः ।
ॐ ह्रीं विष्णुतेजसे शिरसे स्वाहा ।
ॐ क्लीं अमृतानन्दायै शिखायै वषट् ।
ॐ श्रीं दैत्यमालिन्यै कवचाय हुम् ।
ॐ ह्रीं तेजः प्रकाशिन्यै नेत्रत्रयाय वौषट् ।
ॐ क्लीं ब्राह्म्यै वैष्णव्यै रुद्राण्यै अस्त्राय फट् ॥
ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं क्लीं श्रीं सिद्धलक्ष्म्यै नमः इति दिग्बन्धः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aṅganyāsaḥ
ōṃ śrīṃ siddhalakṣmyai hṛdayāya namaḥ ।
ōṃ hrīṃ viṣṇutējasē śirasē svāhā ।
ōṃ klīṃ amṛtānandāyai śikhāyai vaṣaṭ ।
ōṃ śrīṃ daityamālinyai kavachāya hum ।
ōṃ hrīṃ tējaḥ prakāśinyai nētratrayāya vauṣaṭ ।
ōṃ klīṃ brāhmyai vaiṣṇavyai rudrāṇyai astrāya phaṭ ॥
ōṃ śrīṃ hrīṃ klīṃ śrīṃ siddhalakṣmyai namaḥ iti digbandhaḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Aṅga Nyāsa (placement on the body-limbs): Oṃ Śrīṃ Siddhalakṣmyai — salutation — on the heart. Oṃ Hrīṃ Viṣṇutējasē — Svāhā — on the crown of the head. Oṃ Klīṃ Amṛtānandāyai — Vaṣaṭ — on the topknot. Oṃ Śrīṃ Daityamālinyai — Hum — on the armor (kavaca). Oṃ Hrīṃ Tējaḥprakāśinyai — Vauṣaṭ — on the three eyes. Oṃ Klīṃ Brāhmyai Vaiṣṇavyai Rudrāṇyai — Phaṭ — as the weapon-gesture throughout all limbs.
contentBlocks.meanings: aṅga-nyāsaḥ — placement on the body-limbs; hṛdayāya namaḥ — salutation to the heart; śirasē svāhā — offering to the head; śikhāyai vaṣaṭ — vaṣaṭ-call to the topknot; kavachāya hum — hum-mantra for the armor; nētra-trayāya vauṣaṭ — vauṣaṭ to the three eyes; astrāya phaṭ — weapon-mantra phaṭ.

contentBlocks.original:
अथ ध्यानम्
ब्राह्मीं च वैष्णवीं भद्रां षड्भुजां च चतुर्मुखीम् ।
त्रिनेत्रां खड्गत्रिशूलपद्मचक्रगदाधराम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha dhyānam
brāhmīṃ cha vaiṣṇavīṃ bhadrāṃ ṣaḍbhujāṃ cha chaturmukhīm ।
trinētrāṃ khaḍgatriśūlapadmachakragadādharām ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dhyāna (meditation): Meditate upon her as Brāhmī and Vaiṣṇavī, auspicious, six-armed, four-faced, three-eyed, bearing a sword, trident, lotus, discus, and mace — the radiant one adorned with divine ornaments of every kind, wearing yellow garments, holding a mass of effulgence, most excellent — to be meditated as a youthful maiden.
contentBlocks.meanings: brāhmīṃ cha vaiṣṇavīṃ — both as Brāhmī (power of Brahmā) and as Vaiṣṇavī (power of Viṣṇu); bhadrāṃ — auspicious; ṣaḍbhujāṃ — six-armed; chaturmukhīm — four-faced; trinētrāṃ — three-eyed; khaḍga — sword; triśūla — trident; padma — lotus; chakra — discus; gadā — mace; dhārām — bearing; pītāmbara-dharāṃ — wearing yellow garment; nānālaṅkāra-bhūṣitām — adorned with many ornaments; tējaḥpuñja-dharīṃ — holder of a mass of radiance; śrēṣṭhāṃ — most excellent; dhyāyēt — one should meditate; bāla-kumārikām — as a youthful maiden.

contentBlocks.original:
पीताम्बरधरां देवीं नानालङ्कारभूषिताम् ।
तेजःपुञ्जधरीं श्रेष्ठां ध्यायेद्बालकुमारिकाम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pītāmbaradharāṃ dēvīṃ nānālaṅkārabhūṣitām ।
tējaḥpuñjadharīṃ śrēṣṭhāṃ dhyāyēdbālakumārikām ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Wearing yellow garments, the Devī adorned with ornaments of every kind, bearing a mass of radiance, most excellent — one should meditate upon her as a youthful maiden.
contentBlocks.meanings: pītāmbara-dharāṃ — wearing yellow garments; dēvīṃ — the Goddess; nānālaṅkāra-bhūṣitām — adorned with ornaments of all kinds; tējaḥpuñja-dharīṃ — holding a mass of brilliance; śrēṣṭhāṃ — most excellent/supreme; dhyāyēt — should meditate upon; bāla-kumārikām — a youthful maiden.

contentBlocks.original:
अथ स्तोत्रम्
ओङ्कारं लक्ष्मीरूपं तु विष्णुं वाग्भवमव्ययम् ।
विष्णुमानन्दमव्यक्तं ह्रीङ्कारं बीजरूपिणीम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha stōtram
ōṅkāraṃ lakṣmīrūpaṃ tu viṣṇuṃ vāgbhavamavyayam ।
viṣṇumānandamavyaktaṃ hrīṅkāraṃ bījarūpiṇīm ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Oṃkāra — [she is] the form of Lakṣmī; Viṣṇu — [he is] the imperishable Vāgbhava; Viṣṇu — the unmanifest Ānanda; Hrīṃkāra — [she is] the one whose form is the seed-syllable. [To these] I bow.
contentBlocks.meanings: ōṅkāraṃ lakṣmī-rūpaṃ — Oṃkāra [as/is] the form of Lakṣmī; viṣṇuṃ vāgbhavam avyayam — Viṣṇu [as] the imperishable Vāgbhava (the primal Word-origin); viṣṇum ānanda-avyaktaṃ — Viṣṇu [as] the unmanifest Bliss; hrīṅkāraṃ bīja-rūpiṇīm — Hrīṃkāra [as] her whose form is the bīja (seed-mantra).

contentBlocks.original:
क्लीं अमृतानन्दिनीं भद्रां सत्यानन्ददायिनीम् ।
श्रीं दैत्यशमनीं शक्तिं मालिनीं शत्रुमर्दिनीम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
klīṃ amṛtānandinīṃ bhadrāṃ satyānandadāyinīm ।
śrīṃ daityaśamanīṃ śaktiṃ mālinīṃ śatrumardinīm ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Klīṃ — to Amṛtānandinī (she who revels in nectar-bliss), the auspicious one, the bestower of true bliss; Śrīṃ — to Daityaśamanī (the queller of demons), the Śakti, Mālinī, the vanquisher of enemies.
contentBlocks.meanings: klīṃ — the bīja Klīṃ; amṛtānandinī — she who delights in nectar-bliss; bhadrāṃ — auspicious; satyānanda-dāyinīm — bestower of true bliss; śrīṃ — the bīja Śrīṃ; daitya-śamanīṃ — queller/pacifier of the demons; śaktiṃ — Śakti (power); mālinīṃ — Mālinī (garlanded one); śatru-mardinīm — crusher of enemies.

contentBlocks.original:
तेजः प्रकाशिनीं देवीं वरदां शुभकारिणीम् ।
ब्राह्मीं च वैष्णवीं रौद्रीं कालिकारूपशोभिनीम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tējaḥ prakāśinīṃ dēvīṃ varadāṃ śubhakāriṇīm ।
brāhmīṃ cha vaiṣṇavīṃ raudrīṃ kālikārūpaśōbhinīm ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The illuminatrix of radiance, the Devī, the boon-giver, the auspicious doer; Brāhmī, Vaiṣṇavī, Raudrī, the one resplendent in the form of Kālikā — [to her, salutation].
contentBlocks.meanings: tējaḥ-prakāśinīṃ — illuminatrix of radiance; dēvīṃ — the Goddess; varadāṃ — bestower of boons; śubha-kāriṇīm — doer of auspiciousness; brāhmīṃ — Brāhmī (power of Brahmā); vaiṣṇavīṃ — Vaiṣṇavī (power of Viṣṇu); raudrīṃ — Raudrī (power of Rudra); kālikā-rūpa-śōbhinīm — resplendent in the form of Kālikā.

contentBlocks.original:
अकारे लक्ष्मीरूपं तु उकारे विष्णुमव्ययम् ।
मकारः पुरुषोऽव्यक्तो देवी प्रणव उच्यते ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
akārē lakṣmīrūpaṃ tu ukārē viṣṇumavyayam ।
makāraḥ puruṣō'vyaktō dēvī praṇava uchyatē ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: In the letter 'A' [of Oṃ], [she is] the form of Lakṣmī; in the letter 'U', [he is] the imperishable Viṣṇu; the letter 'M' is the unmanifest Puruṣa — the Devī [as a whole] is called the Praṇava (Oṃ).
contentBlocks.meanings: akārē lakṣmī-rūpaṃ — in the letter A, the form of Lakṣmī; ukārē viṣṇum avyayam — in the letter U, the imperishable Viṣṇu; makāraḥ puruṣaḥ avyaktaḥ — the letter M is the unmanifest Puruṣa; dēvī praṇavaḥ uchyatē — the Devī is called Praṇava (Oṃ).

contentBlocks.original:
सूर्यकोटिप्रतीकाशं चन्द्रकोटिसमप्रभम् ।
तन्मध्ये निकरं सूक्ष्मं ब्रह्मरुपं व्यवस्थितम् ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sūryakōṭipratīkāśaṃ chandrakōṭisamaprabham ।
tanmadhyē nikaraṃ sūkṣmaṃ brahmarupaṃ vyavasthitam ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Radiant as crores of suns, of the same splendor as crores of moons — within that [luminous expanse], a subtle mass established as the form of Brahman.
contentBlocks.meanings: sūrya-kōṭi-pratīkāśaṃ — shining like crores of suns; chandra-kōṭi-sama-prabham — with the brilliance equal to crores of moons; tan-madhyē — within that; nikaraṃ — a mass/cluster; sūkṣmaṃ — subtle; brahma-rūpaṃ — of the form of Brahman; vyavasthitam — established/situated.

contentBlocks.original:
ओङ्कारं परमानन्दं सदैव सुरसुन्दरीम् ।
सिद्धलक्ष्मी मोक्षलक्ष्मी आद्यलक्ष्मी नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ōṅkāraṃ paramānandaṃ sadaiva surasundarīm ।
siddhalakṣmī mōkṣalakṣmī ādyalakṣmī namō'stu tē ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Oṃkāra, supreme bliss, ever the beautiful one of the gods — O Siddhalakṣmī! O Mokṣalakṣmī! O Ādyalakṣmī! Salutation to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: ōṅkāraṃ — the syllable Oṃ; paramānandaṃ — supreme bliss; sadaiva — always; sura-sundarīm — beautiful one among the gods; siddha-lakṣmī — Lakṣmī of accomplishment; mōkṣa-lakṣmī — Lakṣmī of liberation; ādya-lakṣmī — the primordial Lakṣmī; namō'stu tē — salutation to you.

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीङ्कारं परमं सिद्धं सर्वबुद्धिप्रदायकम् ।
सौभाग्याऽमृता कमला सत्यलक्ष्मी नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīṅkāraṃ paramaṃ siddhaṃ sarvabuddhipradāyakam ।
saubhāgyā'mṛtā kamalā satyalakṣmī namō'stu tē ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Śrīṃkāra, supreme, accomplished, giver of all intelligence — O one of auspicious fortune, O Nectar, O Kamalā, O Satyalakṣmī! Salutation to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: śrīṅkāraṃ — the bīja Śrīṃ; paramaṃ — supreme; siddhaṃ — accomplished; sarva-buddhi-pradāyakam — giver of all intelligence/wisdom; saubhāgyā — one of auspicious fortune; amṛtā — nectar-form; kamalā — the Lotus one; satya-lakṣmī — Lakṣmī of truth; namō'stu tē — salutation to you.

contentBlocks.original:
ह्रीङ्कारं परमं शुद्धं परमैश्वर्यदायकम् ।
कमला धनदा लक्ष्मी भोगलक्ष्मी नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hrīṅkāraṃ paramaṃ śuddhaṃ paramaiśvaryadāyakam ।
kamalā dhanadā lakṣmī bhōgalakṣmī namō'stu tē ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Hrīṃkāra, supremely pure, giver of supreme lordship — O Kamalā, O Dhanadā, O Lakṣmī, O Bhogalakṣmī! Salutation to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: hrīṅkāraṃ — the bīja Hrīṃ; paramaṃ śuddhaṃ — supremely pure; parama-aiśvarya-dāyakam — giver of supreme lordship/opulence; kamalā — Lotus one; dhanadā — giver of wealth; lakṣmī — Lakṣmī; bhōga-lakṣmī — Lakṣmī of enjoyment; namō'stu tē — salutation to you.

contentBlocks.original:
क्लीङ्कारं कामरूपिण्यं कामनापरिपूर्तिदम् ।
चपला चञ्चला लक्ष्मी कात्यायनी नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
klīṅkāraṃ kāmarūpiṇyaṃ kāmanāparipūrtidam ।
chapalā chañchalā lakṣmī kātyāyanī namō'stu tē ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Klīṃkāra, taking the form of desire, fulfiller of all longings — O Capalā, O Cañcalā, O Lakṣmī, O Kātyāyanī! Salutation to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: klīṅkāraṃ — the bīja Klīṃ; kāma-rūpiṇyaṃ — taking the form of desire/love; kāmanā-paripūrtidam — fulfiller of all desires/longings; chapalā — the fickle/swift one; chañcalā — the ever-moving one; lakṣmī — Lakṣmī; kātyāyanī — Kātyāyanī (fierce aspect of Devī); namō'stu tē — salutation to you.

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीङ्कारं सिद्धिरूपिण्यं सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायकम् ।
पद्माननां जगन्मात्रे अष्टलक्ष्मीं नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīṅkāraṃ siddhirūpiṇyaṃ sarvasiddhipradāyakam ।
padmānanāṃ jaganmātrē aṣṭalakṣmīṃ namō'stu tē ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Śrīṃkāra, the form of accomplishment (siddhi), giver of all accomplishments — O lotus-faced one, O Mother of the World, O Aṣṭalakṣmī! Salutation to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: śrīṅkāraṃ — the bīja Śrīṃ; siddhi-rūpiṇyaṃ — the form of accomplishment; sarva-siddhi-pradāyakam — giver of all siddhis; padmānanāṃ — lotus-faced; jagat-mātrē — O Mother of the World; aṣṭa-lakṣmīṃ — Aṣṭalakṣmī (the eight forms of Lakṣmī); namō'stu tē — salutation to you.

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वमङ्गलमाङ्गल्ये शिवे सर्वार्थसाधिके ।
शरण्ये त्र्यम्बके गौरि नारायणी नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvamaṅgaḻamāṅgaḻyē śivē sarvārthasādhikē ।
śaraṇyē tryambakē gauri nārāyaṇī namō'stu tē ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Auspicious of the auspicious! O Śivā (beneficent)! O accomplisher of all aims! O giver of refuge! O three-eyed Gaurī! O Nārāyaṇī! Salutation to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: sarva-maṅgala-māṅgalyē — O auspicious among all that is auspicious; śivē — O Śivā (the auspicious one); sarva-artha-sādhikē — O accomplisher of all purposes; śaraṇyē — O giver of refuge; tri-ambakē — O three-eyed one; gauri — O Gaurī (the fair, radiant one); nārāyaṇī — O Nārāyaṇī; namō'stu tē — salutation to you.

contentBlocks.original:
प्रथमं त्र्यम्बका गौरी द्वितीयं वैष्णवी तथा ।
तृतीयं कमला प्रोक्ता चतुर्थं सुन्दरी तथा ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prathamaṃ tryambakā gaurī dvitīyaṃ vaiṣṇavī tathā ।
tṛtīyaṃ kamalā prōktā chaturthaṃ sundarī tathā ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The first is Tryambakā-Gaurī; the second is Vaiṣṇavī; the third is declared to be Kamalā; the fourth is Sundarī.
contentBlocks.meanings: prathamaṃ — first; tryambakā gaurī — Tryambakā-Gaurī (three-eyed, radiant); dvitīyaṃ vaiṣṇavī — second is Vaiṣṇavī (power of Viṣṇu); tṛtīyaṃ kamalā prōktā — third is declared to be Kamalā (Lakṣmī); chaturthaṃ sundarī — fourth is Sundarī (the beautiful one, Tripurasundarī).

contentBlocks.original:
पञ्चमं विष्णुशक्तिश्च षष्ठं कात्यायनी तथा ।
वाराही सप्तमं चैव ह्यष्टमं हरिवल्लभा ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pañchamaṃ viṣṇuśaktiścha ṣaṣṭhaṃ kātyāyanī tathā ।
vārāhī saptamaṃ chaiva hyaṣṭamaṃ harivallabhā ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The fifth is Viṣṇuśakti; the sixth is Kātyāyanī; the seventh is indeed Vārāhī; and the eighth is Harivallabhā.
contentBlocks.meanings: pañchamaṃ viṣṇu-śaktiḥ — fifth is Viṣṇuśakti (the power of Viṣṇu); ṣaṣṭhaṃ kātyāyanī — sixth is Kātyāyanī (the fierce warrior-aspect); vārāhī saptamaṃ — seventh is Vārāhī (the boar-headed śakti); aṣṭamaṃ hari-vallabhā — eighth is Harivallabhā (the beloved of Hari/Viṣṇu).

contentBlocks.original:
नवमं खड्गिनी प्रोक्ता दशमं चैव देविका ।
एकादशं सिद्धलक्ष्मीर्द्वादशं हंसवाहिनी ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
navamaṃ khaḍginī prōktā daśamaṃ chaiva dēvikā ।
ēkādaśaṃ siddhalakṣmīrdvādaśaṃ haṃsavāhinī ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The ninth is declared to be Khaḍginī (sword-bearer); the tenth is Devikā; the eleventh is Siddhalakṣmī; and the twelfth is Haṃsavāhinī (she whose vehicle is the swan).
contentBlocks.meanings: navamaṃ khaḍginī prōktā — ninth is called Khaḍginī (sword-bearing goddess); daśamaṃ dēvikā — tenth is Devikā (the divine Goddess); ēkādaśaṃ siddha-lakṣmīḥ — eleventh is Siddhalakṣmī; dvādaśaṃ haṃsa-vāhinī — twelfth is Haṃsavāhinī (swan-mounted).

contentBlocks.original:
उत्तरन्यासः
ॐ श्रीं सिद्धलक्ष्म्यै हृदयाय नमः ।
ॐ ह्रीं विष्णुतेजसे शिरसे स्वाहा ।
ॐ क्लीं अमृतानन्दायै शिखायै वषट् ।
ॐ श्रीं दैत्यमालिन्यै कवचाय हुम् ।
ॐ ह्रीं तेजः प्रकाशिन्यै नेत्रत्रयाय वौषट् ।
ॐ क्लीं ब्राह्म्यै वैष्णव्यै रुद्राण्यै अस्त्राय फट् ॥
ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं क्लीं श्रीं सिद्धलक्ष्म्यै नमः इति दिग्विमोकः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
uttaranyāsaḥ
ōṃ śrīṃ siddhalakṣmyai hṛdayāya namaḥ ।
ōṃ hrīṃ viṣṇutējasē śirasē svāhā ।
ōṃ klīṃ amṛtānandāyai śikhāyai vaṣaṭ ।
ōṃ śrīṃ daityamālinyai kavachāya hum ।
ōṃ hrīṃ tējaḥ prakāśinyai nētratrayāya vauṣaṭ ।
ōṃ klīṃ brāhmyai vaiṣṇavyai rudrāṇyai astrāya phaṭ ॥
ōṃ śrīṃ hrīṃ klīṃ śrīṃ siddhalakṣmyai namaḥ iti digvimōkaḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Uttara Nyāsa (concluding placement): Oṃ Śrīṃ Siddhalakṣmyai — salutation — on the heart. Oṃ Hrīṃ Viṣṇutējasē — Svāhā — on the crown. Oṃ Klīṃ Amṛtānandāyai — Vaṣaṭ — on the topknot. Oṃ Śrīṃ Daityamālinyai — Hum — on the armor. Oṃ Hrīṃ Tējaḥprakāśinyai — Vauṣaṭ — on the three eyes. Oṃ Klīṃ Brāhmyai Vaiṣṇavyai Rudrāṇyai — Phaṭ — as the weapon throughout all limbs.
contentBlocks.meanings: uttara-nyāsaḥ — concluding/subsequent placement; mirrors the aṅga-nyāsa of v4; hṛdayāya namaḥ — for the heart; śirasē svāhā — for the crown; śikhāyai vaṣaṭ — for the topknot; kavachāya huṃ — for the armor; nētra-trayāya vauṣaṭ — for the three eyes; astrāya phaṭ — weapon-gesture.

contentBlocks.original:
अथ फलशृतिः
एतत् स्तोत्रवरं देव्या ये पठन्ति सदा नराः ।
सर्वापद्भ्यो विमुच्यन्ते नात्र कार्या विचारणा ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha phalaśṛtiḥ
ētat stōtravaraṃ dēvyā yē paṭhanti sadā narāḥ ।
sarvāpadbhyō vimuchyantē nātra kāryā vichāraṇā ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Those persons who always recite this excellent stotram of the Devī are liberated from all calamities — there is no doubt in this matter.
contentBlocks.meanings: ētat stotra-varaṃ — this excellent stotram; dēvyāḥ — of the Goddess; yē paṭhanti sadā narāḥ — those persons who always recite; sarva-āpadbhyaḥ vimuchyantē — are liberated from all calamities; na atra kāryā vichāraṇā — no reflection/doubt need be made regarding this.

contentBlocks.original:
एकमासं द्विमासं च त्रिमासं च चतुस्थथा ।
पञ्चमासं च षण्मासं त्रिकालं यः सदा पठेत् ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ēkamāsaṃ dvimāsaṃ cha trimāsaṃ cha chatusthathā ।
pañchamāsaṃ cha ṣaṇmāsaṃ trikālaṃ yaḥ sadā paṭhēt ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: One who recites this [stotram] three times daily for one month, two months, three months, four months, five months, or six months continuously...
contentBlocks.meanings: ēkamāsaṃ — for one month; dvimāsaṃ — for two months; trimāsaṃ — three months; chatuḥ — four; pañchamāsaṃ — five months; ṣaṇmāsaṃ — six months; trikālaṃ — at the three junctures (morning, noon, evening); yaḥ sadā paṭhēt — whoever always recites.

contentBlocks.original:
ब्राह्मणः क्लेशितो दुःखी दारिद्र्यभयपीडितः ।
जन्मान्तर सहस्रोत्थैर्मुच्यते सर्वकिल्बषैः ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brāhmaṇaḥ klēśitō duḥkhī dāridryabhayapīḍitaḥ ।
janmāntara sahasrōtthairmuchyatē sarvakilbaṣaiḥ ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: A Brahmin who is afflicted, suffering, tormented by fear and poverty — is freed from all sins accumulated across thousands of births.
contentBlocks.meanings: brāhmaṇaḥ — a Brahmin (here representing any sincere seeker); klēśitaḥ — afflicted; duḥkhī — suffering; dāridrya-bhaya-pīḍitaḥ — tormented by poverty and fear; janma-antara-sahasra-utthai — arising from thousands of past lives; muchyatē — is freed; sarva-kilbiṣaiḥ — from all sins/impurities.

contentBlocks.original:
दरिद्रो लभते लक्ष्मीमपुत्रः पुत्रवान् भवेत् ।
धन्यो यशस्वी शत्रुघ्नो वह्निचौरभयेषु च ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
daridrō labhatē lakṣmīmaputraḥ putravān bhavēt ।
dhanyō yaśasvī śatrughnō vahnichaurabhayēṣu cha ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The destitute obtains Lakṣmī; the childless becomes blessed with children. One becomes blessed, famous, a destroyer of enemies, and protected from fire and theft.
contentBlocks.meanings: daridraḥ — the destitute; labhatē lakṣmīm — obtains Lakṣmī (prosperity); aputraḥ — the childless one; putra-vān bhavēt — becomes endowed with children; dhanyaḥ — blessed/prosperous; yaśasvī — famous/illustrious; śatru-ghnaḥ — destroyer of enemies; vahni-chaura-bhayēṣu cha — and [protected] in dangers from fire and thieves.

contentBlocks.original:
शाकिनी भूत वेताल सर्प व्याघ्र निपातने ।
राजद्वारे सभास्थाने कारागृहनिबन्धने ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śākinī bhūta vētāla sarpa vyāghra nipātanē ।
rājadvārē sabhāsthānē kārāgṛhanibandhanē ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Against Śākinī demons, ghosts, vetālas, serpents, and tigers; in royal courts, public assemblies, and during imprisonment — [the recitation of this stotram protects].
contentBlocks.meanings: śākinī — a type of malevolent female spirit; bhūta — ghost; vētāla — vampire/animated corpse; sarpa — serpent; vyāghra — tiger; nipātanē — in the falling upon / attack of; rāja-dvārē — in royal courts/gateways; sabhā-sthānē — in public assemblies; kārā-gṛha-nibandhanē — in the bondage of imprisonment.

contentBlocks.original:
ईश्वरेण कृतं स्तोत्रं प्राणिनां हितकारकम् ।
स्तुवन्तु ब्राह्मणाः नित्यं दारिद्र्यं न च बाधते ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
īśvarēṇa kṛtaṃ stōtraṃ prāṇināṃ hitakārakam ।
stuvantu brāhmaṇāḥ nityaṃ dāridryaṃ na cha bādhatē ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: This stotram composed by Īśvara (Śiva) is beneficial to all living beings. Let Brāhmins recite it daily — poverty shall never afflict them.
contentBlocks.meanings: īśvarēṇa kṛtaṃ stōtraṃ — stotram composed by Īśvara (Śiva); prāṇināṃ hita-kārakam — beneficial to all living beings; stuvantu brāhmaṇāḥ nityaṃ — let Brahmins (seekers) recite/praise daily; dāridryaṃ na cha bādhatē — poverty shall not afflict/torment.

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वपापहरा लक्ष्मीः सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायिनीम् ।
साधकाः लभते सर्वं पठेत् स्तोत्रं निरन्तरम् ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvapāpaharā lakṣmīḥ sarvasiddhipradāyinīm ।
sādhakāḥ labhatē sarvaṃ paṭhēt stōtraṃ nirantaram ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Lakṣmī who removes all sins and bestows all accomplishments — practitioners obtain everything by continuous recitation of this stotram.
contentBlocks.meanings: sarva-pāpa-harā — remover of all sins; lakṣmīḥ — Lakṣmī; sarva-siddhi-pradāyinīm — bestower of all accomplishments; sādhakāḥ — practitioners/sādhakas; labhatē sarvaṃ — obtain everything; paṭhēt stōtraṃ nirantaram — by reciting this stotram continuously.

contentBlocks.original:
प्रार्थना
या श्रीः पद्मवने कदम्बशिखरे राजगृहे कुञ्जरे
श्वेते चाश्वयुते वृषे च युगले यज्ञे च यूपस्थिते ।
शङ्खे दैवकुले नरेन्द्रभवने गङ्गातटे गोकुले
सा श्रीस्तिष्ठतु सर्वदा मम गृहे भूयात् सदा निश्चला ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prārthanā
yā śrīḥ padmavanē kadambaśikharē rājagṛhē kuñjarē
śvētē chāśvayutē vṛṣē cha yugalē yajñē cha yūpasthitē ।
śaṅkhē daivakulē narēndrabhavanē gaṅgātaṭē gōkulē
sā śrīstiṣṭhatu sarvadā mama gṛhē bhūyāt sadā niśchalā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: That Śrī who dwells in lotus-forests, on kadamba-treetops, in royal palaces, on elephants, on white steeds, on the sacred bull, on the pair of sacred animals, at the sacrificial post, in conch-shells, in divine lineages, in kings' palaces, on the banks of the Gaṅgā, in Gōkula — may that Śrī dwell in my home always, ever immovable.
contentBlocks.meanings: yā śrīḥ — that Śrī who; padma-vanē — in lotus-forests; kadamba-śikharē — on kadamba-treetops; rāja-gṛhē — in royal palaces; kuñjarē — on elephants; śvētē — on white [horses/vehicles]; āśva-yutē — yoked with horses; vṛṣē cha — on the bull; yugalē — on the pair; yajñē cha yūpa-sthitē — at the sacrificial post in the yajña; śaṅkhē — in conch-shells; daiva-kulē — in divine lineages; narendra-bhavanē — in kings' palaces; gaṅgā-taṭē — on the banks of the Gaṅgā; gōkulē — in Gōkula; sā śrīḥ tiṣṭhatu — may that Śrī remain; sarvadā mama gṛhē — always in my home; bhūyāt sadā niśchalā — ever be immovable.

contentBlocks.original:
या सा पद्मासनस्था विपुलकटितटी पद्मपत्रायताक्षी
गम्भीरावर्तनाभिः स्तनभरनमिता शुद्धवस्त्रोत्तरीया ।
लक्ष्मीर्दिव्यैर्गजेन्द्रैर्मणिगणखचितैः स्नापिता हेमकुम्भैः
नित्यं सा पद्महस्ता मम वसतु गृहे सर्वमाङ्गल्ययुक्ता ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yā sā padmāsanasthā vipulakaṭitaṭī padmapatrāyatākṣī
gambhīrāvartanābhiḥ stanabharanamitā śuddhavastrōttarīyā ।
lakṣmīrdivyairgajēndrairmaṇigaṇakhachitaiḥ snāpitā hēmakumbhaiḥ
nityaṃ sā padmahastā mama vasatu gṛhē sarvamāṅgaḻyayuktā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: She who is seated on the lotus throne, with broad and graceful hips, with eyes wide as lotus-petals, with deep whirling navel, bowing forward under the weight of her bosom, clad in spotless white garments — Lakṣmī, bathed by divine royal elephants pouring from gem-studded golden pitchers — she who is ever lotus-handed: may she ever dwell in my home, the source of all auspiciousness.
contentBlocks.meanings: yā sā — she who is; padmāsana-sthā — seated on the lotus throne; vipula-kaṭi-taṭī — with broad and ample hips; padma-patra-āyata-akṣī — with eyes long as lotus-petals; gambhīra-āvarta-nābhiḥ — with deep whirling navel; stana-bhara-namitā — bowing under the weight of her bosom; śuddha-vastra-uttarīyā — wearing a spotless/pure upper garment; lakṣmīḥ divyaiḥ gajēndraiḥ — Lakṣmī [bathed] by divine royal elephants; maṇi-gaṇa-khachitaiḥ — studded with groups of jewels; snāpitā hēma-kumbhaiḥ — bathed with golden pitchers; nityaṃ sā padma-hastā — always she [is] lotus-handed; mama vasatu gṛhē — may she dwell in my home; sarva-māṅgalya-yukta — endowed with all auspiciousness.

contentBlocks.original: इति श्रीब्रह्मपुराणे ईश्वरविष्णुसंवादे श्री सिद्धलक्ष्मी स्तोत्रम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti śrībrahmapurāṇē īśvaraviṣṇusaṃvādē śrī siddhalakṣmī stōtram ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the Śrī Siddhalakṣmī Stotram in the Īśvara-Viṣṇu dialogue of the Śrī Brahma Purāṇa.
contentBlocks.meanings: iti — thus; śrī-brahma-purāṇē — in the Śrī Brahma Purāṇa; īśvara-viṣṇu-saṃvādē — in the dialogue of Īśvara (Śiva) and Viṣṇu; śrī siddha-lakṣmī stōtram — the Śrī Siddhalakṣmī Stotram; [sampūrṇam — is complete].
